Run UMAP on PSI principal components

Source: R/generics.R, R/dimreduc.R
RunPSIUMAP.Rd

Computes a 2-D UMAP embedding from a PSI PCA reduction (or any other named reduction) using the uwot package. The result is stored as "psi_umap" in the embedded Seurat object.

Usage

RunPSIUMAP(object, ...)

# S4 method for class 'MatisseObject'
RunPSIUMAP(
  object,
  dims = 1:30,
  reduction = "psi_pca",
  n_neighbors = 30L,
  min_dist = 0.3,
  seed_use = 42L,
  verbose = TRUE
)

Arguments

object

A MatisseObject whose embedded Seurat contains the reduction specified by reduction (typically after calling RunPSIPCA).

...

Additional arguments (see RunPSIUMAP).

dims

Integer vector. Which PCA dimensions to use as UMAP input. Default: 1:30.

reduction

Character. Name of the input reduction. Default: "psi_pca".

n_neighbors

Integer. UMAP n_neighbors parameter. Default: 30.

min_dist

Numeric. UMAP min_dist parameter. Default: 0.3.

seed_use

Integer. Random seed for reproducibility. Default: 42.

verbose

Logical. Default: TRUE.

Value

The updated MatisseObject with "psi_umap" reduction.

The input MatisseObject with "psi_umap" added to the embedded Seurat reductions.

See also

RunPSIPCA, FindPSIClusters, PlotPSIDimPlot

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
obj <- RunPSIPCA(obj, n_pcs = 30)
obj <- RunPSIUMAP(obj, dims = 1:20)
} # }
